page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a high-density compute instance powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. It features 192 vCPUs mapped to 96 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz. The system is equipped with 720 GB of dedicated system memory, providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512, AVX_VNNI, and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE and AMX_INT8) for accelerated mathematical and matrix operations. This instance does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware.

Benchmark evaluations place the multi-core performance of this instance in the top tier (top 10%), as demonstrated by stress-ng multi-core CPU tests, PassMark CPU scores, and Geekbench multi-core metrics. Memory bandwidth is exceptionally high, with both small-block cached reads and large-block uncached reads and writes scoring in the top 10%. The hardware excels in parallelized workloads, achieving top-tier results in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression, software compilation with Clang, ray tracing, and database operations. Single-core performance is moderate to strong, with stress-ng single-core tests falling in the middle 50% and Geekbench single-core scores reaching the top 10%.

Given the high density of physical cores and the presence of Intel AMX instructions, the instance is highly efficient for CPU-based machine learning tasks. It achieves top-tier performance in LLM inference for text generation using medium (7B) models and strong performance for large (70B) models. The lack of bundled local storage means storage-heavy workloads must rely on network-attached volumes. The instance represents a highly consolidated resource profile, offering qualitative cost efficiency for massive parallel processing, high-throughput static web serving, Redis databases, and large-scale software compilation pipelines without the overhead of GPU hardware.
description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a compute-heavy instance featuring 192 vCPUs and 720 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 96 physical cores. Operating at 2.3 GHz, it includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) and AVX-512. Benchmarks indicate top-tier multi-core CPU performance, exceptional memory bandwidth, and high throughput for database operations and static web serving. While single-core performance is average to strong, its multi-threaded capabilities excel in software compilation, ray tracing, and CPU-based LLM inference. Lacking local storage and GPUs, this instance is optimized for highly parallelized, compute-intensive workloads such as large-scale compilation, Redis deployments, and matrix-heavy mathematical simulations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 is a compute-heavy instance with 192 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8581C processor, delivering top-tier multi-core performance for parallel workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-192 features 192 vCPUs, 720 GB RAM, and Intel Xeon processors, optimized for high-throughput multi-core workloads.
tagline: A high-density, multi-core compute platform engineered for parallel processing, advanced matrix operations, and high-throughput database workloads without dedicated GPUs.
bullet_points: Powered by a 192-vCPU Intel Xeon 8581C processor with 720 GB of RAM.
Supports Intel AMX and AVX-512 for accelerated matrix and vector calculations.
Delivers top-tier multi-core CPU performance and high memory bandwidth.
Excellent fit for CPU-based LLM inference, software compilation, and Redis.
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included.
